what_it_does: This file is a ledger record for a design-source image asset: it stores YAML metadata (id, address, channel, path, blob URI, MIME type, dimensions, source, author session, creation time, links) and a one-line body labeling the JPEG as SOURCE design ground-truth for landing-mockups/p-2.
observations: YAML frontmatter defines a channel-memory image ledger entry
The asset is an image/jpeg referenced by blob://b2:8ebe24f6c7c16bc0e0c4470d9e296277
Width and height are recorded as 0
Body text reads 'SOURCE design ground-truth — landing-mockups/p-2.jpg'
standouts: Identified as design ground-truth for a landing-mockup image
Blob-addressed asset with zero recorded dimensions
Empty links and minimal descriptive content
concerns: w and h are 0, suggesting missing or uncomputed image dimensions
No alt text, caption, or description of the actual visual content
links array is empty, so no explicit relationships are recorded
notes: A sparse ledger stub for a landing-mockup JPEG in the design-source channel, with placeholder dimensions and no linked relations.
questions: What visual content does landing-mockups/p-2.jpg actually show?
Why are the width and height recorded as 0?
Is the blob resolvable and does it match this metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to catalog a design-source image asset; it does that, but the zero dimensions and empty links mean the record is incomplete rather than wrong.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register a landing-page mockup image as an authoritative, addressable design-source artifact within the company's channel memory system.
contribution: Provides a stable, addressable metadata record that links a JPEG blob to the design-source channel under the landing-mockups path, enabling retrieval and provenance tracking for that asset.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory ledger stub for the image asset landing-mockups/p-2.jpg in the design-source channel, referencing a JPEG blob with zero recorded dimensions and no links.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a landing-mockup image as design ground-truth material with a resolvable blob address.
